name: European TTF Natural Gas
summary: Day 87 (Monday May 25 — Europe-ex-UK traded LIVE; UK FTSE/LME closed for Spring Bank Holiday). **TTF GAS €46.63/MWh, -4.2% 1D LIVE — DEAL-RELIEF + 3 LNG VESSELS CROSSED HORMUZ. BUT THE +25% YoY STRUCTURAL FLOOR HOLDS (+4.4% MoM; vs ~€31.20 pre-crisis baseline). This is the European leg of the deal-anticipation tape (paper prices the deal): TTF fell with Brent (sub-$100), urea (-7.4%), India equities (+1.3%). The structural physical premium is NOT buying the deal as done — Qatar Ras Laffan 17% liquefaction force majeure is a 3-5yr recovery, NOT contingent on Iran deal architecture, so the YoY floor is durable. C4 paper-physical non-confirmation: even on a clean deal, the physical gas/LNG cost stack bleeds out over 6-12 weeks, not in a session — the 3 LNG vessels crossing Hormuz are a thin first trickle, not a reopening. Trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' remains NOT ACTIVATED (€46.63 vs €70 = €23 gap, further from threshold than May 22). B1 context: the EU formally ADOPTED a Hormuz-disruption sanctions framework May 22 (CONFIRMED, no designations yet) — upgrades the prior single-source Al Jazeera flag; a Hormuz-sanctions architecture being built at the moment a US-Iran deal would reopen the strait is a transatlantic-divergence cross-current for European gas. Henry Hub $3.02 FROZEN (US Memorial Day — NYMEX did not settle). Reversibility: shallow on deal-anticipation (-€2 to -€5/MWh, realised today); structural floor at ~€40-42 absent durable Qatar capacity return.** PRIOR (May 24): **TTF GAS €48.69/MWh FRIDAY MAY 22 CLOSE (-1.46% 1D, +56% ABOVE PRE-CRISIS €31.20 BASELINE). QATAR RAS LAFFAN FORCE MAJEURE 17% LIQUEFACTION PERSISTS AS STRUCTURAL FLOOR; NORWAY MAINTENANCE EASE HOLDING. THE STRUCTURAL PHYSICAL PREMIUM IS NOT BUYING THE PAKISTAN DEAL-ANTICIPATION TAPE — TTF AT €48.69 IS 56% ABOVE BASELINE EVEN AS BRENT DECLINED -5% WEEKLY. TRIGGER 'TTF CROSSES €70/MWh SUSTAINED 5 SESSIONS' REMAINS NOT ACTIVATED (€48.69 vs €70 THRESHOLD = €21 GAP). NO EU EMERGENCY MEASURES SURFACED IN MAY 22-24 WINDOW; AL JAZEERA MAY 22 SINGLE-SOURCE 'EU MOVES TO SANCTION IRAN' STORY STILL UNCORROBORATED BY REUTERS/POLITICO/EURACTIV/LE MONDE PRIMARY — REPORTED LOW-CONFIDENCE.** **Henry Hub US Natural Gas $3.02 Friday close** (+0.1% 1D, +15.6% MoM, +3.6% 3M) — LNG-basis story; Cheniere $240.85 LNG-basis structural bid intact. **The structural-physical pricing**: Europe's TTF premium is durable through Pakistan deal-track because Qatar Ras Laffan recovery is 3-5 years, NOT contingent on Iran deal architecture. **Reversibility**: shallow on deal-anticipation (-€2 to -€5/MWh); structural floor at €40-€42 absent durable Qatar capacity return. PRIOR (May 23): TTF GAS €48.69/MWh MAY 22 CLOSE — FLAT-TO-EASING; QATAR RAS LAFFAN FM 17% LIQUEFACTION PERSISTS BUT NORWAY MAINTENANCE EASE HOLDING. EU SANCTIONS IRAN STORY (AL JAZEERA MAY 22 SINGLE-SOURCE) = REPORTED LOW-CONFIDENCE; NOT CORROBORATED BY EUROPEAN-SIDE PRIMARY (REUTERS/FT/POLITICO EUROPE/EURACTIV).** Per markets dossier C8: TTF held into weekend close at €48.69 — Norway Troll maintenance eased; GCC LNG halt persists; price drift back up modestly vs Thursday's €47.69. **Goldman tail scenario ~€72/MWh remains comfortably above current print** but price acceleration trajectory has clearly turned over past two weeks — Europe pricing 'deal-anticipation + structural-LNG-disruption' coexisting. **Trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' remains NOT ACTIVATED** (current €48.69 vs €70 threshold). **Structural floor intact**: Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery; ECB Lagarde 'new energy shock' framing carries forward; European inventory refill window tightening into summer. **No European emergency visible**: German DW + France 24 did not surface new emergency measures in this search window. **Henry Hub US Natural Gas $2.91 May 22 close** (-3.7% 1D, +11.2% MoM, -2.6% 3M) — Friday's drop = EIA storage report (92 Bcf injection in-line, holds +140 Bcf surplus to seasonal norm 3rd consecutive week); 1M premium = LNG-basis story. **Cheniere Energy $240.85** (+0.2% 1D, -6.1% 1M, +8.1% 3M) — LNG-basis structural bid intact. PRIOR (May 22): **TTF GAS €47.69/MWh (-3.48% 1D / +7.38% 1M / +30.7% YoY) — SHALLOW RETRACEMENT DESPITE BRENT -5.3% = EUROPEAN ENERGY MARKET PRICING NUCLEAR-TRACK UNRESOLVED + QATAR RAS LAFFAN 17% FORCE MAJEURE AS STRUCTURAL, NOT CONTINGENT ON THE LEAKED TEXT.** Norway Troll maintenance eased; GCC LNG halt since March persists; price dropped on fresh Pakistan-deal skepticism then partial retracement. **The cleanest European signal**: TTF retracement is SHALLOW (-2%) despite Brent -5.3% — European energy market pricing nuclear-track unresolved and Qatar Ras Laffan 17% force majeure as STRUCTURAL, not contingent on the leaked text. **Goldman tail scenario of ~€72/MWh remains comfortably above current print** but the price acceleration trajectory has clearly turned over past two weeks. **Trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' remains NOT ACTIVATED** (Day 0 of 5, current €47.69 vs €70 threshold — further from threshold than May 19). **Structural floor intact**: Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery; ECB Lagarde 'new energy shock' framing carries forward; European inventory refill window tightening into summer. **No EUROPEAN EMERGENCY visible**: German DW and France 24 did not surface in this search window, consistent with European energy markets reading the deal-anticipation tape rather than emergency-preparing. **Henry Hub US Natural Gas $3.03 (+0.3% 1D, +15.8% 1M, +1.4% 3M)** — LNG-basis story; Qatar Ras Laffan FM 17% liquefaction persists. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **B4 EUROPEAN TTF GAS €50.16/MWh MONDAY — FLAT VS FRIDAY €50.85 CLOSE** (within daily noise). Past month +18.26%; +42.75% YoY; ~20% of global LNG supply disrupted since war began. **Friday's +6.7% one-day move that May 17 brief flagged has NOT extended Monday — the gas complex is treating the weekend kinetic-preparation tape as already priced.** Trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' remains at Day 0; ~€20/MWh from activation threshold. **The TTF tape is the cleanest 'not capitulating yet' signal in the European cascade complex** — paired with VIX sub-22 (18.43) + KOSPI rally (+0.6%), the vol-pricing structure is *not* gapping despite maximal-pressure weekend tape (UAE Barakah, Saudi drone, Trump-Netanyahu, 'clock is ticking'). Cheniere's 'astounded prices not higher' framing carries forward. Bloomberg/ICE TTF data corroborates. **Structural floor intact**: Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery; ECB Lagarde 'new energy shock' framing carries forward; European inventory refill window tightening into summer. **PRIOR (May 17): TTF GAS €50.85/MWh MAY 15 CLOSE (+6.7% 1D, ~+34% 1M, ~+57% 3M vs pre-war €32.39) — even HIGHER than May 15 brief's €49.86 working figure per markets dossier Section F.** Per markets dossier C-section: LNG complex feels Qatar squeeze + Hormuz LNG-tanker risk + April rebuild paused. ~85% crisis. **Markets closed Sunday — TTF resumes Monday open.** **Monday May 18 binary**: TTF €50.85 sustained vs retrace on no-LNG-incident weekend. Goldman tail scenario of ~€72/MWh remains comfortably above current print but the trajectory has clearly turned — Europe is no longer in 'alarm nor relief' mode but in 'creeping pricing-in' mode. **Trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' remains NOT ACTIVATED but is approaching activation territory.** Cheniere CCO Hormuz disruption framing carries forward. ECB Lagarde 'new energy shock' May 6 speech framing structural-energy-inflation regime carries forward; DAX -2.1% Friday on European industrials capex hit. Henry Hub US Natural Gas $2.96 (+2.3% 1D, +11.8% 1M, -2.3% 3M) — Hormuz-LNG bid spillover via TTF arbitrage. **PRIOR (May 16):** B4 TTF €49.86/MWh MAY 15 (+4.63% 1D, +60%+ 3M vs pre-war €31.20) — THIRD CONSECUTIVE DAILY INCREASE AND SECOND-CONSECUTIVE >1% MOVE; HIGHEST SINCE APRIL** (CONFIRMED — Trading Economics, OilPriceAPI, Investing.com, Euronews). Driver chain: ongoing Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure on ~17% of liquefaction capacity (recovery 'in years not months'); Hormuz tensions; Europe's pre-winter storage rebuild. Pre-war Europe sourced 12-14% of LNG from Qatar; structural picture has not changed. **The Goldman tail scenario of ~€72/MWh remains comfortably above today's print, but the trajectory has clearly turned over the past week** — Europe is no longer in 'alarm nor relief' mode (yesterday's framing) but in 'creeping pricing-in' mode. **Trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' remains NOT ACTIVATED** but the +4.63% acceleration that crossed €48 and €49 in one session is the cleanest pricing-in signal of the week. Markets dossier C2 sees ~1/5 of global LNG supply disrupted since war began as the structural anchor; TTF is the most direct read. **ECB Lagarde 'new energy shock' May 6 speech framing structural-energy-inflation regime carries forward** — DAX -2.1% on May 15 (HeidelbergCement -7.16%, Siemens -4.94%, MTU Aero -4.87%) shows capex-intensive industrials being hit on inflation/yield-shock + Iran stall. Henry Hub US Natural Gas $2.96 (+2.3% 1D, +11.8% 1M, -2.3% 3M) — Hormuz-LNG bid spillover via TTF arbitrage. Cheniere (LNG) $241.84 +0.3% 1D. PRIOR (May 15): **B7 TTF €47.61/MWh MAY 14 (+1.47% 1D, +15% 1M, +34.65% YoY vs pre-war €31.20) — CREEPING UP, SECOND CONSECUTIVE DAILY INCREASE, BUT REMAINS WELL BELOW THE GOLDMAN-ESTIMATED +130% HORMUZ-DISRUPTION TAIL SCENARIO (~€72/MWh).** European-energy channel on neither alarm nor relief. **EIA confirmed earlier this month that international LNG prices have risen amid the Strait of Hormuz closure that has cut around one-fifth of global LNG supply** (markets dossier B7). European gas storage rebuilding ahead of winter remains the tail-risk; the 1.47% daily move is consistent with global-supply-tightness drift rather than acute Hormuz-induced spike. **Trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' NOT ACTIVATED.** Henry Hub US Natural Gas $2.92 (+0.9% 1D, +10.3% 1M, -3.7% 3M). Cheniere (LNG) $241.08 (+0.7% 1D, -5.9% 1M, +10.3% 3M). Structural offsets intact: Hormuz throughput ~30 ships/day per Aramco; Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery; European inventory refill window tightening into summer. ECB has not characterized Hormuz risk as systemic. Trump-Xi joint statement reduces nominal tail-risk on rhetoric; physical Qatar LNG outage continues to provide structural floor. **B4 TTF €46.63/MWh MAY 13 (-0.11% 1D, basically flat at the €46 range yesterday's brief noted; ~+47% from pre-war €31.20 baseline) — CONFIRMS QUIET; sustained-elevated mode, neither alarm nor relief**. No fresh European Council action priced; Cheniere CCO Hormuz disruption framing carries forward. Container freight FBX13 (China/East Asia → Mediterranean) $4,179/FEU week ending May 12 (-5% w/w) carries forward. European-energy-channel neither escalating nor relieving = sustained-elevated mode. Trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' NOT ACTIVATED. **Henry Hub US Natural Gas $2.89 (+0.8% 1D, +10.6% 1M, -4.8% 3M)** — Hormuz-LNG bid spillover via TTF arbitrage; Cheniere supply pricing pulling US gas higher (~80% crisis). **Cheniere (LNG) $239.38 (-2.0% 1D, -7.6% 1M, +9.6% 3M)** — Q1 $3.5bn hedge loss + LNG infrastructure trade-unwind on Hormuz-tail-risk diminishment + Trump-Xi opening reduces crisis-premium short-term (~85% crisis-trade-unwind). Structural offsets intact: (a) Hormuz throughput 2-5 ships/day per Aramco; (b) Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery; (c) European inventory refill window tightening into summer. ECB has not characterized Hormuz risk as systemic. PRIOR (May 13): Markets dossier notes ~€44 baseline May 8 → €46.23 May 11 = +5% on news escalation. **PARTIAL DECOUPLING WITH BRENT NARROWING:** Brent +1.3% 1D May 12 vs TTF +4.74% 1D — TTF playing catch-up to crisis pricing. TTF remains BELOW €70+ cascade-intensity threshold; trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' NOT ACTIVATED. **Henry Hub US Natural Gas +0.7% 1D / +11.6% 1M** — indirect Qatar LNG diversion → European TTF demand → US LNG export pull (markets B-section). Cheniere (LNG) $240.70 (+0.5% 1D, -9.1% 1M) — Q1 2026 $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM (long-term LNG hedged at pre-crisis prices); CCO Anatol Feygin: 'astounded that global gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' US LNG players SHORT the war via hedge books. **Structural offsets intact:** (a) Hormuz throughput DETERIORATED to 2-5 ships/day per Aramco CEO Nasser (vs ~6/day May 10); (b) European inventory refill window tightening into summer (storage at 5-year seasonal low); (c) Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. ECB has not characterized Hormuz risk as systemic. **FT framing:** prolonged Hormuz disruption 'could continue to push energy prices higher and reignite inflation pressures' — TTF the most direct read.

current.price: €46.63/MWh May 25 LIVE (Europe-ex-UK traded; deal-relief + 3 LNG vessels crossed Hormuz). Henry Hub $3.02 FROZEN (Friday May 22 — US Memorial Day, NYMEX did not settle).
current.delta_1d: -4.2% 1D LIVE (deal-anticipation re-price; paper leg of the same tape as Brent <$100, urea -7.4%)
current.delta_3m: +25% YoY vs pre-crisis €31.20 baseline — the STRUCTURAL FLOOR; durable because Qatar Ras Laffan 17% liquefaction force majeure is a 3-5yr recovery, NOT contingent on the Iran deal.
current.vs_2022_crisis: still well below 2022 crisis levels (€100+); BELOW the €70+ cascade-intensity threshold (€23 gap, further from threshold than May 22); the -4.2% deal-relief move re-prices the tail, not the +25% YoY structural floor.

price_snapshots.trigger: Deal-relief: US-Iran deal progress + 3 LNG vessels crossed Hormuz (first thin trickle) re-priced the European gas tail premium DOWN — the European leg of the deal-anticipation tape (Brent sub-$100, urea -7.4%, India +1.3%).
price_snapshots.delta_context: -4.2% 1D LIVE. But +4.4% MoM / +25% YoY — the structural floor holds (Qatar Ras Laffan 17% FM, 3-5yr recovery, NOT deal-contingent). C4 non-confirmation: physical gas/LNG cost stack bleeds out over 6-12 weeks even on a clean deal, not in a session.

recent_signals.headline: **TTF €46.63/MWh, -4.2% 1D LIVE — DEAL-RELIEF + 3 LNG VESSELS CROSSED HORMUZ; BUT THE +25% YoY STRUCTURAL FLOOR HOLDS. THE €70-SUSTAINED TRIGGER REMAINS NOT ACTIVATED (€23 GAP).** (1) **Mechanism**: TTF fell -4.2% as the European leg of the deal-anticipation tape — US-Iran deal progress + reports of 3 LNG vessels crossing Hormuz (a thin first trickle) re-priced the European gas tail premium DOWN, co-moving with Brent (sub-$100), urea (-7.4%), and India equities (+1.3%) in the LIVE-traded clean-room session (US/UK markets closed). (2) **But the structural floor holds**: +4.4% MoM, +25% YoY vs the ~€31.20 pre-crisis baseline. Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) force majeure is a 3-5yr recovery — NOT contingent on Iran deal architecture — so the YoY premium is durable. C4 paper-physical non-confirmation: even on a clean signed deal, the physical gas/LNG cost stack bleeds out over 6-12 weeks, not in a session; 3 LNG vessels is not a reopening. (3) **Trigger**: 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' remains NOT ACTIVATED — €46.63 vs €70 = €23 gap, FURTHER from threshold than May 22's €48.69. (4) **B1 context**: the EU formally ADOPTED a Hormuz-disruption sanctions framework May 22 (CONFIRMED across multiple European outlets — upgrades the prior single-source Al Jazeera flag), enabling travel bans / asset freezes but with NO designations yet. A Hormuz-sanctions architecture being erected at the moment a US-Iran deal would reopen the strait and waive US oil sanctions is a transatlantic-divergence cross-current. (5) **Henry Hub $3.02 FROZEN** (US Memorial Day — NYMEX did not settle; do NOT read as a live Monday move). **Reversibility**: shallow on deal-anticipation (-€2 to -€5/MWh, realised today); structural floor ~€40-42 absent durable Qatar capacity return.
sources: TradingEconomics / ICE (TTF €46.63/MWh -4.2% 1D LIVE; +4.4% MoM, +25% YoY; 3 LNG vessels crossed Hormuz — markets dossier Section A/F)
Markets dossier 2026-05-25 STRUCTURAL NOTE (Europe-ex-UK LIVE; UK FTSE/LME closed; Henry Hub FROZEN)
Markets dossier 2026-05-25 C4 (paper-physical non-confirmation — physical bleeds out over 6-12 weeks)
Brussels Signal / Marine Insight / Express Tribune May 22 (EU Hormuz sanctions framework adopted — intel B1)
recent_signals.verification: TTF €46.63 / -4.2% 1D = CONFIRMED LIVE MARKET DATA (Europe-ex-UK traded); 3 LNG vessels crossed Hormuz = REPORTED; +25% YoY structural floor = CONFIRMED (Qatar Ras Laffan FM ongoing); €70-sustained trigger NOT ACTIVATED = CONFIRMED ABSENCE; EU Hormuz sanctions framework = CONFIRMED (multi-source European), no designations yet; Henry Hub $3.02 = FROZEN Friday (US holiday data-status caveat).

recent_signals.headline: **TTF €50.36/MWh TUESDAY (essentially flat vs Monday €50.16); SIX-WEEK PEAK AT €51.2 ON HORMUZ UNCERTAINTY — TRIGGER €70-SUSTAINED REMAINS DAY 0, ~€20/MWh FROM THRESHOLD.** No European emergency framing today — FT and BBC institutional caution holds; Deutsche Welle would carry European energy-emergency framing if TTF approached €70, no DW alarm at €50.36. **Cheniere's European-storage-141-cargoes-below-5y-low LNG basis structural** — gas-market remains tight but not in emergency. Cheniere raised 2026 EBITDA guide to $7.25-7.75B (Oilprice / 24/7 Wall St) — US LNG fungibility into European-emergency-supply is structurally bid; Hormuz reopening bearish for this trade, but reopen-timeline 2H26.
sources: Intel dossier 2026-05-19 A13; Markets dossier C4c F

European-energy-channel neither escalating nor relieving. **Henry Hub US Natural Gas $2.89 (+0.8% 1D, +10.6% 1M, -4.8% 3M)** — Hormuz-LNG bid spillover via TTF arbitrage (~80% crisis). **Cheniere (LNG) $239.38 (-2.0% 1D, -7.6% 1M, +9.6% 3M)** = Q1 $3.5bn hedge loss + LNG infrastructure trade-unwind on Hormuz-tail-risk diminishment (~85% crisis-trade-unwind). Structural offsets intact: Hormuz 2-5 ships/day; Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery; European inventory refill window tightening into summer.

MOU 'both sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision priced Qatar Ras Laffan reopening expectation. TTF still below €70+ cascade-intensity threshold; structural offsets (Hormuz closed, inventory tight, Ras Laffan 3-5yr recovery) intact.
